steps.title: Identify the critical phrase and its meaning
steps.content:
The underlined portion contains “or I o'erween [presume] to think so.” The word “o'erween” means to presume or overestimate.

By inserting this phrase right after claiming he might comfort the king, Camillo is adding a self-correction: he's uncertain whether his presence will actually provide the comfort he claims.

This acknowledgment of his own doubtful assumption is the key signal.

steps.title: Find the choice matching this acknowledgment
steps.content:
Choice D states the underlined portion “conveys Camillo's recognition that one benefit his presence may afford the king of Sicily is merely speculative”—this directly matches the self-doubt Camillo expresses.

The other choices either claim the passage bolsters confidence (A) or focus on the wrong king or person (B, C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
